articles.title: ARE DESIGNERS HAPPY? OUR NEW “STATE OF THE DESIGNER” REPORT AIMS TO FIND OUT
articles.summary: The "State of the Designer" report by Figma explores the impact of changing work styles on designers' job satisfaction, productivity, and relationships. Despite the challenges of the past three years, including a shift to remote work, 69% of designers report greater job satisfaction, with individual contributors feeling the most positive.

articles.title: GOOGLE CHROME MATERIAL YOU REDESIGN WIDELY ROLLING OUT
articles.summary: Google Chrome's 2023 redesign for its 15th birthday introduces the Material You design language with rounded corners and taller elements like the tab strip and address bar. Significant changes include the removal of the lock icon from the Omnibox, replaced by a site control icon, and a focus on legibility with outline-style icons and reorganized menu items.

articles.title: INTERVIEW WITH HUMANE COFOUNDER
articles.summary: Imran Chaudhri, cofounder of Humane, speaks about AI Pin, an AI-powered game-changing device announced last week. The former Apple designer discusses everything from privacy and partnerships with "frenemies" to the end of the App Store.

articles.title: 10 DARK PATTERNS IN UX DESIGN AND HOW TO AVOID THEM
articles.summary: Common dark patterns include confirm shaming, fake urgency, bait and switch, privacy suckering, nagging, sneaking, disguised ads, intentional misdirection, the roach motel pattern, preselection, price comparison prevention, trick questions, sneaking into the basket, friend spam, and forced continuity. To avoid dark patterns, designers should prioritize transparency, user control, and ethical design practices, fostering positive user experiences and long-term customer loyalty.

articles.title: BUSINESSES DON’T HIRE DESIGNERS FOR THEIR GOOD WORK
articles.summary: As a designer, it is your job to contribute to the business's success. However, you should sometimes ask yourself if you want the business to grow and who is profiting from it.

articles.title: THE SMART HOME COMPANY THAT'S ON THE RYSE 🚀
articles.summary: If you haven’t heard of RYSE yet, you will soon. The story of the smart home COMPANY that started out pitching on Dragons Den and receiving two offers [10] is about to become known nationwide, as their product rolls out in +100 Best Buy stores.

articles.title: Don’t miss your chance to invest at $1.25/share.
UNDERSTANDING ACCESSIBILITY
articles.summary: A collection of accessibility guidance that helps designers use colors, typefaces, media, and layout in a way to create accessible experiences.

articles.title: MONASPACE
articles.summary: A superfamily of five fonts for coding with three variable axes: Neon a neo-grotesuqe sans, Argon a humanist sans, Xenon a slab serif, Radon a handwriting font, and Krypton a mechanical sans. All of them use "texture healing", a feature that improves the spacing between letters to make reading an easier and more pleasurable experience.

articles.title: DESIGN WEBSITES WITH AI (FIGMA PLUGIN)
articles.summary: Musho converts prompts into near-complete, developer-ready websites with simple layouts, excellent copy, and stunning imagery.

articles.title: HOW THE GUARDIAN DESIGNED ITS THOUGHTFUL NEW LONG-FORM MAG
articles.summary: A new magazine from The Guardian dedicated to long-form journalism hits newsstands on November 11. When designing the magazine, a lot of attention was given to packaging its large volume of text – clocking in at 55,000 words – to make the reading experience as relaxing as possible, from body type size to column widths.

articles.title: DESIGN VALIDATION GUIDE: PLAN, PROCESS, EXAMPLES
articles.summary: With design validation, you can ensure your design is user-friendly and considers the business goals. In this practical guide, you will learn how to formulate a design validation plan, tools, and common mistakes to avoid.

articles.title: HOW TO USE CHATGPT IN YOUR DESIGN WORK
articles.summary: AI-assisted design is poised to change design forever, but most people are stuck on the beginner level. To become proficient, they should try to use it for rapid prototyping of conversational interfaces, user persona generation, generating and iterating on design ideas, copywriting and content creation, usability testing scripts, accessibility and inclusive design, personalized user experience, and microscopy.

articles.title: CREATING INCLUSIVE AI: A STRENGTHS-BASED APPROACH TO ACCESSIBLE DESIGN
articles.summary: AI-powered systems often overlook people with varying cognitive abilities and disabilities. Researchers are looking for ways to create more accessible intelligent systems that empower users across the spectrum of human ability.

articles.title: FONTS FOR RENDERING LINES AND BARS FROM DATA
articles.summary: Google Fonts now provides two open-source fonts by Dmitry Ivanov (Linefont and Wavefont) that let you make simplified, small to medium line and bar charts based on data. These might be useful when you want to embed small charts in body text.
